content: Human brains evolved active memory-as-learning mechanisms where recalling a memory is not just a retrieval step but an update that incorporates new contextual information, a principle that can resolve a key flaw in 2024–2025 SOTA active learning agent memories: catastrophic forgetting of high-value old exploratory experiences when updating with new environment data. This can be implemented by adding a human-like "recall-and-update" plasticity step to curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agents, where every time an agent retrieves an episodic memory to plan a new curious exploration, it adjusts that memory’s priority weight based on how well it predicted the current observation.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
trigger_topic: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
